skill.name: gdpr-dsgvo-expert
skill.description: Provides structured GDPR and German DSGVO guidance for compliance reviews, DPIA planning, data subject rights workflows, audit preparation, and privacy governance.
skill.summary: Assess GDPR and DSGVO compliance with structured privacy audit guidance.
skill.author: alirezarezvani

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography, command execution, environment access, and network reconnaissance patterns, but review found these are false positives from GDPR terminology, Markdown code fences, and placeholder resource names. No executable command invocation, secret access, network activity, data exfiltration, or prompt injection attempt was found in the reviewed files. The remaining risk is low because the skill contains broad legal guidance and placeholder references, so users should verify legal advice with qualified counsel.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography matches occur in GDPR, DSGVO, DPIA, compliance, guide, and documentation text. They do not identify cryptographic functions, hashing code, encryption configuration, or security-sensitive implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are legal, audit, or placeholder documentation. I found no cryptographic API usage or implementation context at these locations.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are Markdown Formatting
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are Markdown code fences or inline file names in documentation. They do not execute commands and do not contain user-controlled shell input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is Markdown syntax and resource-list text only. No script invokes a shell, backticks are not inside Ruby code, and no command execution path exists.

low_findings.title: Static Environment Access Finding Is Legal Text
low_findings.description: The environment access finding points to GDPR Article 10 content about criminal conviction data. It is not configuration loading and does not access environment variables or secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is a bullet in a GDPR legal framework. It contains no process environment reference, configuration library use, or secret handling.

low_findings.title: Static Network Reconnaissance Findings Are Privacy Program Text
low_findings.description: The network reconnaissance findings point to organizational measures and privacy risk management wording. They do not describe scanning, port discovery, host enumeration, or network requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is a privacy audit checklist and KPI list. I found no network tooling, destination host, socket use, or reconnaissance workflow.

content.value_statement: Teams need structured privacy reviews that connect legal duties with operational evidence. This skill guides GDPR and German DSGVO assessments, DPIA planning, rights handling, and audit preparation.
actual_capabilities: Maps GDPR and DSGVO requirements to practical compliance review areas.
Helps plan Data Protection Impact Assessments for high-risk processing.
Structures data subject rights workflows and response documentation.
Outlines international data transfer review steps under GDPR Chapter V.
Supports audit readiness for policies, records, DPIAs, and governance evidence.
Provides privacy program metrics for continuous improvement discussions.
limitations: It does not provide formal legal advice or replace qualified counsel.
It does not include complete referenced templates or implementation files.
It cannot verify factual compliance without user-provided evidence.
It may require updates for new regulator guidance or case law.

use_cases.title: Prepare an Internal GDPR Audit
use_cases.description: Create an audit scope, evidence request list, and review structure for processing activities.

use_cases.title: Plan a DPIA for New Processing
use_cases.description: Assess DPIA triggers, privacy risks, mitigation measures, and consultation needs.

use_cases.title: Review Data Subject Rights Handling
use_cases.description: Check intake, identity verification, deadlines, decisions, and response documentation.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Compliance Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a quick readiness review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this processing activity for GDPR and DSGVO compliance gaps. Focus on legal basis, notice, retention, rights, and accountability evidence.

prompt_templates.title: DPIA Planning
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for a structured impact assessment.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan a DPIA for this project. Identify DPIA triggers, likely risks to individuals, required evidence, mitigation options, and open questions.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Evidence Request
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for internal audit preparation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a GDPR audit evidence request list for this scope. Group evidence by governance, records of processing, DPIAs, contracts, rights handling, security, and transfers.

prompt_templates.title: Cross-Border Transfer Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for Chapter V transfer analysis.
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess this international data transfer scenario. Identify the transfer mechanism, transfer risk factors, supplementary measures, documentation gaps, and decision points.

output_examples.input: Assess whether our marketing analytics processing needs a DPIA.
output_examples.output: The response would classify DPIA triggers, list missing facts, assess likely privacy risks, and recommend whether a full DPIA is needed.

output_examples.input: Prepare evidence requests for a data subject rights audit.
output_examples.output: The response would produce grouped evidence requests for intake logs, verification steps, response timing, exemptions, fulfillment records, and complaint handling.

output_examples.input: Review our processor transfer to a non-EEA provider.
output_examples.output: The response would outline transfer mechanism checks, SCC documentation, transfer risk assessment topics, supplementary safeguards, and unresolved legal questions.

best_practices: Provide concrete processing details, data categories, systems, countries, and parties.
Ask for evidence lists and decision records, not only general compliance summaries.
Have qualified legal counsel review conclusions before regulatory or contractual reliance.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the output as final legal advice.
Do not omit sensitive facts such as special category data or international transfers.
Do not use it to create compliance claims without supporting evidence.

faq.question: Can this skill replace a Data Protection Officer?
faq.answer: No. It can support analysis and documentation, but it cannot replace statutory DPO duties or professional judgment.

faq.question: Does it cover both GDPR and German DSGVO topics?
faq.answer: Yes. It focuses on EU GDPR duties and German implementation considerations, including BDSG-related context.

faq.question: Can it decide whether a DPIA is required?
faq.answer: It can structure the assessment and identify decision points. Final decisions should use current regulator guidance and legal review.

faq.question: Does it include ready-to-use templates?
faq.answer: The skill references templates and tools, but the reviewed package mostly contains guidance and placeholder files.

faq.question: Can it review international data transfers?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide Chapter V review topics, transfer mechanisms, risk factors, and supplementary measure analysis.

faq.question: What inputs improve the results?
faq.answer: Processing purposes, legal basis, data categories, systems, recipients, retention periods, countries, contracts, and existing policies improve accuracy.
